zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe於1301年出生在英國賀福爾易亞迪斯利時，他的父親Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae33歲，母親Sybella Corbett26歲。他與Jane de Poyntz在英國英格蘭賀福爾易亞迪斯利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1343年在他的家鄉享年43歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀福爾金頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe于1301年出生在英国賀福爾易亞迪斯利时，他的父亲Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae33岁，母亲Sybella Corbett26岁。他与Jane de Poyntz在英国英格兰賀福爾易亞迪斯利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1343年在他的家乡享年43岁，埋葬在英国英格兰賀福爾金頓。

nameMeanings.name: Baskerville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: Combe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Michel.
Scottish (Midlothian and East Lothian): from Gaelic Mac Thom ‘son of Thom’, see Thomas . It is also a shortened form of McCombe .
English: variant of   Coombe  .

variants.name: Thomas
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Flemish, Danish, Greek, West Indian (mainly Haiti and Jamaica), and African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Thomas, of Biblical (New Testament) origin, from Aramaic t’ōm’a, a byname meaning ‘twin’. It was borne by one of the disciples of Christ, known for his scepticism about Christ's resurrection (John 20:24–29). The Th- spelling is organic, the initial letter of the name in the Greek New Testament being a theta. The English pronunciation as t rather than th- is the result of French influence from an early date. In Britain, the surname is widely distributed throughout the country, but especially common in Wales and Cornwall. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Toma and Tuma , Albanian Toma and Thoma , and Slavic surnames listed in 3 below), and their patronymics and other derivatives (e.g. Polish Tomaszewski and Slovenian Tomažič; see Tomazic ). In France, this surname is most common in the Vosges and Brittany. The name Thomas is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Machan , Mammen , and Oommen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Thomas (see 1 above) as a surname.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Tomas , Tomasz, and Tomaś, Sorbian Tomaš (see also 4 below), Croatian Tomaš and Tomas , Slovenian Tomaš and Tomaž, Czech and Slovak Tomáš, all meaning ‘Thomas’.

variants.name: Comeau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Camille, Alphie, Emile, Fernand, Herve, Jacques, Marcel, Monique, Murielle, Normand.
French:
topographic name from a diminutive of come, a variant of combe ‘narrow valley, ravine’ (see Combe ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe was born in 1301, in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England, his father, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, was 33 and his mother, Sybella Corbett, was 26. He married Jane de Poyntz in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died about 1343, in his hometown, at the age of 43, and was buried in Kington, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe est né en 1301, à Eardisley, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Sybella Corbett, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Jane de Poyntz à Eardisley, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1343, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Kington, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe 1301 in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae 33 und seine Mutter Sybella Corbett 26. Er hat in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom Jane de Poyntz geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1343 im Alter von 43 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Kington, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe è nato il nel 1301 nel Eardisley, Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Sybella Corbett, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Jane de Poyntz nel Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1343, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Kington, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combeが1301年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアエアルディスリーで生まれた時，父Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combaeは33歳，母Sybella Corbettは26歳であった。イギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアエアルディスリーでJane de Poyntzと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1343年に故郷で43歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアキントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe(이)가 1301년 영국 허퍼드실르 에르디슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Sybella Corbett(은)는 26세였다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 에르디슬리에서 Jane de Poyntz(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1343년 그의 고향에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 킨그턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe nasceu em 1301, em Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Sybella Corbett, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane de Poyntz em Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1343, em sua cidade natal, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Kington, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe родился в 1301 г. в местности эардисли, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Sybella Corbett, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane de Poyntz в местности эардисли, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1343 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности кингтон, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Baskerville - Lord of Eardisley and Combe nació en 1301, en Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Sybella Corbett, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Jane de Poyntz en Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1343, en su pueblo, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Kington, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
